event_blocks.summary: The segment begins in a modern, stainless steel kitchen where Gordon Ramsay, sporting spiky blonde hair and a black t-shirt, collaborates with Tom Holland, who is clad in a beige button-up shirt and a maroon 'BERO' apron. Initially, they discuss the genesis of their collaboration, with Tom recounting a past incident involving a backflip and a broken nose, while Gordon humorously cautions against similar stunts. The scene transitions to a title card showcasing 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay', sponsored by HEXCLAD, before returning to the kitchen where they prepare an 'amazing sandwich' with an 'incredible marinade', highlighting the prominently displayed gold 'BERO' cans. Their conversation spans various topics, from Tom's busy schedule, including 'prepping for Spider-Man 4' and 'working on BERO', to his unexpected path to acting, which began with ballet lessons and a 'lame street dance show'. Gordon probes into Tom's early career, expressing surprise at his unexpected success and the challenges of transitioning into darker roles. They delve into the intricacies of BERO's unique brewing process, emphasizing its non-alcoholic nature and the difficulty of achieving its distinct flavor, with Gordon admitting his initial skepticism towards non-alcoholic drinks was overturned by BERO's authentic taste. As they cook, Gordon shares his culinary philosophy, comparing recipes to 'chemistry equations' and emphasizing the importance of identifying what's missing, while Tom recounts his extensive search for the perfect non-alcoholic beer during BERO's development. They also discuss the importance of balance and avoiding burnout, with Tom reflecting on the impact of lockdown and the value of personal life alongside professional success, and the origins of his close bond with his brother Sam, a talented chef. The segment culminates with Gordon and Tom assembling a dish, layering ingredients like shredded cabbage, creamy sauce, and fried chicken onto buns, while discussing the emotional impact of 'Billy Elliot' and the importance of family dinners. Throughout the segment, the kitchen is filled with the sounds of chopping, sizzling, and their engaging dialogue, punctuated by the frequent appearance of BERO cans, symbolizing their collaborative venture.

Restaurant Interior.description: A dimly lit dining area with hanging pendant lights, wooden tables, and a sophisticated ambiance, briefly shown as part of the setting context.

Tom Holland.description: A man with curly brown hair, wearing a beige button-up shirt and a maroon apron with 'BERO' printed on it, standing with arms crossed, observing Gordon Ramsay, and gesturing expressively during their conversation. He speaks with a clear voice, sharing personal anecdotes and reflections on a musical, often with a humorous or thoughtful tone.

BERO Cans.description: Several gold-colored cans labeled 'BERO' are placed on the kitchen counter, with one being picked up by Gordon Ramsay during the cooking process, indicating their presence and potential use in the segment.

Title Card.description: A graphic overlay with the text 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay' and 'SPONSORED BY HEXCLAD', featuring a stylized egg yolk splash design, briefly appearing to brand the segment.

Gordon Ramsay.description: A man with spiky blonde hair, wearing a black t-shirt, actively assembling a dish by layering shredded cabbage, sauce, and fried chicken onto buns, using a spoon and his hands. He engages in conversation with Tom Holland, asking questions in a distinct, direct voice, and picks up a 'BERO' can during the segment.

Kitchen Environment.description: A modern, stainless steel kitchen with professional appliances including an induction cooktop, deep fryer, and shelves stocked with bottles and tools, featuring a wooden cutting board, metal trays, and ingredients like buns, lime, shredded cabbage, and fried chicken.

Flashback Scene.description: A black-and-white image of two men shaking hands on a golf course, framed with a yellow border and egg yolk splashes, appearing as Tom Holland describes his family's shared history and traditions.

raw_segments: The segment opens with an upbeat, driving background music as Gordon Ramsay, dressed in a black t-shirt, stands in a modern, well-lit kitchen, posing the question, "What happened when Spider-Man and an Iron Man come together?" Tom Holland, wearing a beige button-up shirt and a maroon apron with 'BERO' printed on it, animatedly responds, "We make sandwiches." Gordon enthusiastically agrees, then humorously instructs, "Please, no backflips," to which Tom replies, "No backflips," and recounts a past incident where he broke his nose doing a backflip in Colombia. The energetic music swells as a title card appears, displaying 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay', sponsored by HEXCLAD, with a stylized egg yolk splash. Gordon formally welcomes viewers to "Scrambled in the stunning Gordon Ramsay Academy," introducing Tom Holland as "an incredible actor, an amazing young man, the founder of BERO." Tom thanks Gordon, and they engage in a friendly conversation, with Tom detailing his busy schedule, including "prepping for Spider-Man 4" and "working on BERO." Gordon then asks if Tom ever imagined becoming a superhero, to which Tom emphatically replies, "No, not at all," explaining he was always a "good athlete" but on the "B team." Gordon playfully challenges this, but Tom maintains his stance, mentioning his brothers were on the A-teams. The scene cuts between close-ups of both men as they interact, with Tom looking engaged and occasionally glancing at Gordon, while Gordon gestures toward a counter filled with ingredients, including onions, limes, and several prominently displayed gold 'BERO' cans. The setting briefly transitions to a professional kitchen with industrial equipment and then to a dimly lit restaurant, before returning to the bright, modern kitchen where Gordon announces they will make an "amazing sandwich" with an "incredible marinade," as Tom holds a lime, ready to begin the collaborative cooking demonstration.

The segment opens in a bright, modern stainless steel kitchen where Gordon Ramsay, a man with spiky blonde hair wearing a black t-shirt, animatedly speaks while holding a gold-colored BERO can. He discusses starting with buttermilk and unique ingredients to tenderize chicken, then transitions to actively chopping cabbage on a wooden board. Tom Holland, with curly brown hair, wearing a beige button-up shirt and a maroon apron with 'BERO' printed on it, appears beside him, listening intently and responding with expressive gestures. Holland explains their goal was to create a non-alcoholic drink, synonymous with sobriety, that removes the typical 'what non-alcoholic drinks do you have?' question at bars, even playfully mentioning 'BERO Hero Spider-Man.' As Ramsay coats chicken pieces in flour and places them on a tray, he interjects, admitting he wasn't a massive fan of non-alcoholic drinks previously but BERO has 'definitely, definitely changed that perception' because 'it does taste like the real thing.' Holland then asks, 'How hard was that?' before elaborating on BERO's 'very different process,' brewed in a unique way by their brew master, Grant Wood, using a special yeast they believe they're the only company to use. The two continue their collaborative cooking, with Holland chopping cabbage, as he concludes by stating he tried 'every single non-alcoholic beer under the sun' during BERO's development, all within the clean, professional kitchen environment where several BERO cans are prominently displayed.

The segment opens in a modern, well-lit stainless steel kitchen where Gordon Ramsay, a man with spiky blonde hair and a black t-shirt, is intensely focused, coating chicken pieces in flour on a metal tray. His deep, authoritative voice is heard stating, "I'm biased, but it really is the best," likely referring to the food or a product. Beside him, Tom Holland, with curly brown hair and wearing a maroon 'BERO' apron, concentrates on chopping cabbage. Tom's slightly higher-pitched, clear voice responds, "It's good to be biased," before asking Gordon if he realized how difficult it would be to achieve such a unique flavor. Gordon then moves to a pan, stirring ingredients with a wooden spoon, explaining that recipes are "more like chemistry equations" and that for him, the process was about identifying what he "didn't like and what I was missing." Tom interjects with "Yeah" and "Science," acknowledging Gordon's points. Gordon vividly describes a "long process" where for a long time, the product "tasted like sh*t" and was "minging," to which Tom agrees, "it's a process, right?" Gordon, gesturing expressively, recounts how "one day he just turned it on" and "turned in three different skewers, which we all fell in love with," praising "Grant Woody" as "fantastic." Tom affirms, "And so should we, it's delicious," with Gordon responding, "Thank you." Throughout, several gold-colored BERO cans are prominently displayed on the counter, with Gordon handling one at a point, as the camera alternates between close-ups of their faces and wider shots, capturing their energetic and collaborative culinary efforts amidst the sounds of their engaging dialogue and subtle kitchen activity.

In a bright, modern stainless steel kitchen, Gordon Ramsay, with spiky blonde hair and a black t-shirt, is actively coating chicken in flour on a metal tray. He asks Tom Holland, who is chopping cabbage on a wooden cutting board while wearing a beige button-up and a maroon 'BERO' apron, "How did it start?" Tom, speaking with a conversational tone, explains he "never wanted to be an actor" but "always loved performing" and "dancing," having attended a "Saturday school" on weekends. As Gordon gestures expressively and moves to stir ingredients in a pan with a spatula, Tom continues chopping, asking if he should "put a whole chili in there." Gordon, in his authoritative voice, confirms it's "gonna be a hot one" and instructs him to put "both" the red and green chilies in. The dynamic interaction continues as Tom recounts performing in a "lame street dance show" at "White Lodge, in the park, ballet school," to which Gordon reacts with "Yes." Tom further details how the headmaster of the school suggested he audition for "Billy Elliot" by "complete chance," a revelation that prompts Gordon to exclaim, "Stop it." Close-ups capture their focused expressions and hands at work amidst the professional kitchen setup, where several gold 'BERO' cans are prominently displayed, one briefly handled by Gordon. Tom concludes, explaining he "went and auditioned" and they "must have liked me," sending "ballet teachers" to his "very, very masculine..." as the segment ends, maintaining a fast-paced, collaborative cooking atmosphere with the sounds of chopping and stirring accompanying their clear dialogue.

In a modern, well-lit stainless steel kitchen, Gordon Ramsay, a man with spiky blonde hair in a black t-shirt, is intensely focused on coating chicken pieces in flour and placing them into a pan, generating consistent sizzling sounds. Alongside him, Tom Holland, with curly brown hair, wearing a beige button-up shirt and a maroon apron with 'BERO' printed on it, diligently chops cabbage on a wooden cutting board, his actions accompanied by distinct chopping sounds. Tom narrates his personal journey, recounting how he had teachers teach him ballet at school, which "did not go down very well," and how after two years of auditioning, he got a part. He explains that his parents viewed it as an "amazing exercise to learn new skills" and become a professional. Gordon interjects, expressing surprise at the unexpected turn of events, saying, "We have no idea that... next moment you're reacting," and later, "I love that," as Tom describes an agent seeing his show and offering representation. Tom continues, detailing his first audition out of the gate for a film that "did really well," leading to American reps and a career where he "never looked back." Gordon then probes further, asking if that "first part was quite a dark story" and if the transition from "zero into that" was hard, noting it "wasn't an easy picking." Throughout their conversation, Gordon handles one of the gold-colored BERO cans prominently displayed on the counter, possibly adding its contents to the pan, while Tom maintains his concentration on chopping, occasionally glancing up to engage with Gordon. The camera alternates between wide shots of the collaborative cooking and close-ups of their detailed culinary actions.

The segment opens with an overhead shot of Gordon Ramsay, a man with spiky blonde hair, actively frying food in a deep fryer, using tongs amidst the constant sizzling sounds of cooking. The scene transitions to a side view as steam rises from the fryer, with Tom Holland, wearing a maroon apron with 'BERO' printed on it, standing beside him, observing and engaging in conversation. Tom, with his curly brown hair, speaks in a clear, narrative voice about the excitement of being on a movie set as a kid, finding it "so exciting and impressive" and how "all these amazing creative people doing different things that are all contributing to the bigger picture." Gordon interjects with a distinct "Yeah" in agreement. Tom continues, explaining how he was "so distracted by my now love for movies" that he "wasn't really that hung up on how intense the experience was," to which Gordon adds, "The actual yeah, amazing." The camera cuts to a wider shot of the modern, stainless steel kitchen where both men work side by side. Gordon, chopping fresh herbs on a wooden cutting board, then asks Tom in a questioning tone about handling pressure, "And in terms of nerves, how do you hide those? How do you how do you how do you deal with that kind of pressure because you make it seem seamless now." Tom, seen slicing cabbage and squeezing a lime into a small container, responds that the best advice he ever got was from a director who asked him to describe the feeling of queuing up for a rollercoaster. As Tom handles a gold-colored BERO can and mentions putting "a bit of mustard in there," Gordon confirms with "Absolutely." Tom then struggles with opening something, possibly the BERO can, remarking, "God, I was not about to ask you to open that for me there. I was going to give every ounce of strength to open," followed by a distinct *thud/clatter* and a laugh. The bright kitchen features various cooking tools, ingredients, and multiple BERO cans. The segment concludes with Gordon turning to Tom, continuing their dynamic conversation as they prepare components for a dish, likely a burger, with buns and shredded purple cabbage visible.

The minute opens with Tom Holland, wearing a beige button-up shirt and a maroon apron labeled 'BERO', speaking directly to the camera in a modern stainless steel kitchen. He gestures animatedly while discussing feelings of excitement and nervousness, stating, "I've just spent my entire life kind of brainwashing myself to believe that when I'm nervous... I'm just really excited." As he speaks, the rhythmic sound of chopping is audible as he begins preparing green vegetables on a wooden cutting board. The scene then cuts to a wider shot, revealing Gordon Ramsay in a black t-shirt, also actively chopping herbs on a separate cutting board, working side-by-side with Tom. The professional kitchen is well-equipped with stainless steel appliances and various ingredients, including several gold-colored BERO cans on the counter. Gordon Ramsay, with a distinct accent, then engages, gesturing expressively and occasionally picking up a BERO can, remarking, "Food runs in the family brilliantly... Sam being a prolific chef." Tom Holland responds with a clear "Yes" and continues chopping and squeezing lime juice. Gordon then questions Tom about his diet, asking, "How do you deal with that on the set? Have you stopped picking? Are you more cautious about what you're eating? Are you focusing more on your diet?" Tom replies, "It depends on what I'm working on, like we're going onto Spider-Man now... which means I've got to be really careful with what I eat so I can stay fit and healthy." The camera alternates between close-ups of their hands chopping and wider shots of their collaborative cooking, accompanied by a light, upbeat background music that begins towards the end of the segment. A brief title card appears, displaying 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay' and 'SPONSORED BY HEXCLAD' with a stylized egg yolk splash. The segment concludes with Tom Holland focused on his work, while Gordon Ramsay continues to engage with the camera and ingredients.

The minute opens in a modern, stainless steel kitchen, filled with the sounds of chopping and light background music, where Gordon Ramsay, with spiky blonde hair and a black t-shirt, actively chops herbs on a wooden cutting board. He speaks with an enthusiastic and appreciative tone, praising someone named Sam, stating, "Be on top of my game every day. What's been so lovely about Sam is, you know, you've been very helpful for Sam, which I'm hugely appreciative of." Nearby, Tom Holland, in a beige button-up and a maroon 'BERO' apron, prepares ingredients like cabbage and lime, adding his supportive comments about Sam, "He's naturally talented with food by the way." Gordon agrees, "Yeah, he really does have a gift," as Tom emphatically adds, "there's a natural connect that it's just it's obvious. And he was born to cook." Both men handle gold-colored BERO cans visible on the counter. Gordon, gesturing expressively, reveals Sam is "doing a cookbook" and enthusiastically describes how Sam cooks "amazing meals" for his family, calling it "glorious." Tom, engaged, asks about "favorite dishes?" Gordon lists "an amazing katsu curry" and "sticky pork bao bun," noting Sam "does everything from scratch," to which Tom responds, "Love that." A title card briefly cuts in, displaying 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay' and 'SPONSORED BY HEXCLAD' with an egg yolk splash design, before returning to the kitchen. Gordon then describes an "indulgent" meal, asking if it's "a curry?" Tom answers, "My favorite, I have to say, is a chicken biryani. But with dough from potatoes," as they continue their collaborative cooking. The professional, clean, and well-lit kitchen setting is consistent, with a brief glimpse of a dimly lit restaurant interior also shown.

The video opens with an overhead shot of a modern kitchen counter, where food is being deep-fried in a BERO deep fryer, accompanied by the distinct sound of sizzling. Ingredients like chopped herbs, lime, and BERO cans surround the fryer. The scene quickly transitions to a close-up of Tom Holland, wearing a beige shirt and a maroon apron with 'BERO' printed on it, holding a black cloth and speaking animatedly. Gordon Ramsay, in a black t-shirt, enters the frame, gesturing emphatically as he describes a "chicken dish" where "he peels the grapes" for a "really creamy sauce," to which Tom reacts with a "Wow." The two men interact dynamically in the stainless steel kitchen, with Tom chopping cabbage and Gordon moving around the counter, occasionally tasting food and reacting with expressive gestures. Gordon asks Tom how long it takes to "peel those grapes," and Tom replies, "Oh mate, he sits there all day doing it." Gordon then suggests, "So get your other person to start peeling these grapes." Gordon picks up a BERO can while posing a hypothetical question about superpowers, asking what power Tom would have "on the back of all those incredible movies." Tom, handling ingredients, responds that he "would have the ability to teleport" to make his life easier. They engage in lively conversation, recalling a "summer where we were on the same flight every second weekend," with Gordon adding, "Every other weekend," and Tom mentioning they were "sitting opposite each other" enjoying "little glass of champagne." A title card briefly appears, displaying 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay' and 'SPONSORED BY HEXCLAD' with a stylized egg yolk splash design, branding the show. Gordon continues to recount how "once I got the bottle back from the hostess," they would "sit there talking," adding, "I never told you what happened like the third or fourth time. I just finished filming. I sat down, got a board track suit." The atmosphere remains energetic and collaborative, with both men focused on the cooking process and their animated interaction.

The video opens in a modern, stainless steel kitchen, brightly lit and featuring professional appliances like a deep fryer and induction cooktop. Tom Holland, wearing a beige button-up shirt and a maroon 'BERO' apron, sips from a metallic cup as Gordon Ramsay, in a black t-shirt, animatedly gestures and speaks with an energetic British accent, recounting a story about taking a sleeping tablet and Tom keeping him awake. Tom, with a surprised British accent, interjects, "No way!" as Gordon continues to blame him for staying up for four hours. The two are positioned around a central kitchen counter laden with ingredients including buns, lime, shredded cabbage, and several gold-colored BERO cans. Gordon picks up a BERO can, examines it, and gestures emphatically while tasting food from a small bowl, explaining they were having "such a great chat." Tom responds with enthusiasm, also handling a BERO can and taking a drink, before resuming his work with ingredients in a large metal bowl. Their interaction is lively and collaborative, filled with laughter and exchanged gestures. Gordon jokes about "landing in four hours," prompting Tom to laugh and exclaim, "That is hilarious!" They share a "Cheers, mate" as they clink cans. Gordon then playfully suggests Tom use "James" in future movies, referencing James Bond, to which Tom responds, "There's speculation at the minute. We'll keep it to a minimum for now." The scene briefly cuts to a dimly lit restaurant interior with wooden tables and pendant lights, before returning to the kitchen where Tom is seen chopping cabbage and Gordon observes closely. Gordon quips, "Shaken, not BERO'd?" leading Tom to comment that "It doesn't look the same in a martini glass," followed by shared laughter. The segment concludes with both men laughing and engaging in animated conversation, highlighting their camaraderie and shared focus on the cooking process, all set to upbeat background music and the sounds of kitchen activity.

The video opens in a modern stainless steel kitchen where Tom Holland, wearing a beige button-up shirt and a maroon apron with 'BERO' printed on it, is conversing. Gordon Ramsay, in a black t-shirt, enters the frame, gesturing animatedly as he asks Tom, "What's gonna happen to your life if you do? First of all, you'd love that opportunity, right?" Tom, speaking with a British accent, responds enthusiastically about his acting career, stating, "Oh, dude, I mean, every young British actor, it's the pinnacle of... working in our industry... I could never have dreamed to have the career that I've had." Gordon acknowledges with "Sure." The camera pans to show the kitchen setup: a tray of fried chicken, buns, lime, shredded cabbage, and several gold BERO cans arranged on the counter, one of which Gordon picks up. Tom, focused on preparation, chops cabbage and cracks an egg into a large metal bowl. He then asks Gordon, "And how many of these should I put in?" Gordon, giving instructions, explains they'll "dress it lightly," emphasizing "Nothing worse with a sort of heavy cloy... We want it nice and light." Tom confirms, "Two tablespoons," as Gordon details adding "fresh lime juice, a little bit of lime zest... Salt, pepper, a little touch of hot sauce," before they "start putting this beautiful bad boy together." Gordon then passionately explains the benefits of an ingredient, likely from the BERO can, stating, "whilst this is nice and sort of fruity... the tenderization it does in the chicken is off the charts." Tom, surprised, asks, "Really?" Gordon confirms, "Yeah, it really, really good." Tom then poses an insightful question, "Do you find that the lack of presence of alcohol makes a difference in that process?" Gordon elaborates on the "flavor profile" and "lack of alcohol." The segment briefly shows a title card with 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay' and 'SPONSORED BY HEXCLAD' over a stylized egg yolk splash, before returning to the kitchen where the two continue their dynamic cooking demonstration.

The segment opens in a modern, stainless steel kitchen, where Gordon Ramsay, in a black t-shirt, animatedly gestures and speaks with an intense expression. He discusses drinking in moderation and praises a "super ice cold" refreshing drink, likely from the gold-colored BERO cans visible on the counter, one of which he picks up. Tom Holland, wearing a beige button-up shirt and a maroon apron with 'BERO' printed on it, is focused on chopping cabbage and handling ingredients, interjecting with agreements like "Yeah" and "Exactly," and expressing thanks. Gordon continues, reflecting on not being a "pint guy" but appreciating the drink's "off the charts" quality. The conversation shifts to finding balance and avoiding burnouts, with Gordon asking about the moment of realizing the need to "time out, recuperate, refresh, relax, and charge those batteries," noting how rare such balance is, especially in one's 20s. Tom responds, revealing he spent a significant amount of time with Gordon during lockdown, which was the first time he was "forced to take a break." Gordon interjects about putting the "passport, put away," to which Tom confirms, "Yeah, literally," then reflects on "fame is such a fickle thing" and how quickly it happened for him. Throughout, close-ups show Gordon's expressive delivery and Tom's precise food preparation, including shredded cabbage being mixed in a metal bowl, all set against the backdrop of professional kitchen appliances and the consistent sounds of chopping and general kitchen activity.

The segment opens in a modern stainless steel kitchen, where Tom Holland, wearing a beige button-up shirt and a maroon 'BERO' apron, speaks animatedly with expressive hand gestures. He reflects on how quickly things can happen and disappear, stating, "So I felt this need to constantly be working... when COVID happened and I was forced to stay at home, it made me realize just how important, you know, your personal life is as well as your professional life." He continues, explaining his new approach to "finding ways to, you know, enjoy the fruits of my labor perhaps." Tom then humorously recounts his past cooking attempts, admitting, "I've never really been a cook. It's always been Sam," and describing how he would "literally hit every day, watch one of your videos, go to the supermarket, buy the ingredients, come, make an absolute dog's dinner of it." As he mentions "Sam comes back and makes it," Gordon Ramsay, in a black t-shirt, enters the frame smiling, engaging warmly with Tom, who gestures towards him. Gordon playfully mimics a cooking motion and responds, "I love that." Gordon then speaks about a "sibling brotherhood," noting, "the connect is incredible... it's like this pack." A brief black-and-white flashback, framed with a yellow border and egg yolk splashes, shows two men shaking hands on a golf course as Tom explains the origin of this bond: "Our parents. Every night, family dinners... No phones, no TVs on, sit down and talk." The scene returns to the kitchen, where Gordon is now actively mixing ingredients in a metal bowl, while Tom watches and gestures toward the counter, which is cluttered with golden BERO cans, buns, lime, and shredded cabbage, indicating an ongoing cooking demonstration. Tom concludes, "And I think after we left school..."

The segment opens in a modern stainless steel kitchen where Gordon Ramsay, in a black t-shirt, is actively assembling a dish, layering shredded cabbage onto buns. Tom Holland, wearing a beige button-up and a maroon 'BERO' apron, stands nearby, observing with arms crossed. Tom's voice is heard discussing how friends live close by, humorously adding that the only reason they visit his house is for the air conditioning and his "nice and cool" kitchen. As Gordon continues to layer creamy sauce and fried chicken onto the buns, the camera alternates between close-ups of his hands and wider shots of the two men. Gordon, with a distinct, direct voice, then asks Tom if he managed to "get your head around that story of Billy Elliot," calling it "quite groundbreaking." Tom responds, confirming he saw the musical, and Gordon interjects, calling it "an amazing piece of work." Tom continues, describing how "there wasn't a dry eye in the theater." Gordon then asks if Tom has "been to Newcastle," to which Tom replies, acknowledging the "devastating" nature of the setting. Gordon further probes about the impact of playing such a part, while Tom, gesturing expressively, reflects on being "so caught up in the idea of being young and on the West End" and performing for thousands. He then shares his grown-up perspective, appreciating the musical as being "for the everyday man," describing a "dad crying while he's singing to his son about the hardships of going back to work when he's on strike." Throughout this conversation, a can labeled 'BERO' is visible on the counter, and Gordon picks one up. The dynamic and collaborative atmosphere persists as Gordon places the final piece of fried chicken, completing the dish.

event_blocks.summary: In a modern stainless steel kitchen, Tom Holland, clad in a beige button-up and a maroon 'BERO' apron, and Gordon Ramsay, in a black t-shirt, collaborate on assembling gourmet chicken sandwiches. Gordon meticulously layers shredded cabbage, sauce, and twice-dredged, tender fried chicken onto buns, describing the components including bib and butter lettuce, hot sauce, and a jalapeño slaw bound with light mayo. Tom, initially observing with crossed arms, engages with the process, remarking on the sandwich's delicious and gorgeous appearance. They share playful banter about eating in a car before both taking bites of the finished sandwich, exclaiming delight, with Tom highlighting the slaw's contribution and the chicken's quality. Gordon picks up a gold 'BERO' can from the counter as they discuss Tom's "Lip Sync Battle" performance, which garnered 177 million views and became a viral sensation, a topic Tom humorously notes haunts him more than his "Spider-Man" movies. Tom recounts agreeing with Zendaya to "take it easy" for the performance, but rehearsals escalated with a "rain machine" and "umbrella thing," leading to a desire for fishnets, wigs, and quick changes, with the framed fishnets now at the Met. Gordon acknowledges Tom's "annoying" success and "great job," while Tom's dad initially worried about him becoming "too famous too quickly" and doing "extra TV shows." A flashback scene shows two men shaking hands on a golf course in black and white, framed with yellow borders and egg yolk splashes. The segment, branded 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay' and 'SPONSORED BY HEXCLAD,' concludes with Gordon inviting viewers to "berobrewing.com" to find where they can purchase the product, with multiple BERO cans visible on the counter.

Flashback Scene.description: A black-and-white image of two men shaking hands on a golf course, framed with a yellow border and egg yolk splashes, suggesting a nostalgic or thematic connection to the current segment.

Title Card.description: A graphic overlay with the text 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay' and 'SPONSORED BY HEXCLAD', featuring a stylized egg yolk splash design, briefly appearing to brand the segment visually without direct audio.

Tom Holland.description: A man with curly brown hair, wearing a beige button-up shirt and a maroon apron with 'BERO' printed on it, initially observing with arms crossed, then engaging in playful conversation, expressing delight with "Oh my word!" and "That is delicious" after taking a bite of the sandwich, and praising the "gorgeous" chicken and "slaw" in a distinct voice.

Kitchen Environment.description: A modern, stainless steel kitchen with professional appliances, shelves stocked with bottles and tools, featuring a wooden cutting board, metal trays, and ingredients like buns, lime, shredded cabbage, and fried chicken, serving as the consistent backdrop for the lively conversation and food tasting.

BERO Cans.description: Several gold-colored cans labeled 'BERO' are placed on the kitchen counter, with one being picked up by Gordon Ramsay, and the brand "berobrewing.com" is explicitly mentioned by Gordon at the end, indicating their sponsorship and connection to the segment.

Gordon Ramsay.description: A man with spiky blonde hair, wearing a black t-shirt, actively engaging in conversation with a clear, enthusiastic voice, describing sandwich ingredients in detail, reacting to the taste with delight, and picking up a gold-colored BERO can while concluding the segment and promoting "berobrewing.com."

raw_segments: The video opens in a modern stainless steel kitchen, where Tom Holland, wearing a beige button-up shirt and a maroon 'BERO' apron, stands with arms crossed, observing Gordon Ramsay. Gordon, in a black t-shirt, is actively assembling a dish—layering shredded cabbage, sauce, and fried chicken onto buns—while engaging in animated conversation with Tom. Tom, speaking in a clear, conversational voice, discusses the importance of "going against his union." Gordon, with his distinct, energetic voice, then asks Tom if he ever thought he'd be part of "one of the biggest final moments of your entire career, Lip Sync Battle." Tom animatedly responds, "No, I did not," explaining that "people didn't know that I could dance" and he "kept that quiet," before declaring, "I came out with a bang." Gordon echoes his sentiment, "You came out with a bang, I mean, Jesus." As they interact, Tom gestures expressively, recounting how he and Zendaya had initially agreed to "take it easy" for the performance, planning "not to do anything crazy." Gordon interjects with a surprised "What?" Tom continues, detailing how the creative team pitched an "umbrella thing," and rehearsals grew "crazier and crazier" with a "rain machine" coming in, leading him to want to "wear fishnets and do this" and use "a wig and a quick change." During this, several gold 'BERO' cans are visible on the counter, one of which Gordon picks up. The scene briefly cuts to a title card reading 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay' and 'SPONSORED BY HEXCLAD', featuring a stylized egg yolk splash design. A flashback appears as a black-and-white image of two men shaking hands on a golf course, framed with yellow borders and egg yolk splashes. The segment maintains a lively, dynamic atmosphere with both men moving around the kitchen, occasionally laughing and gesturing, as Gordon continues to prepare the dish and Tom reacts with enthusiasm, with Gordon asking about Tom's brother just before the minute ends.

The segment opens in a bright, modern stainless steel kitchen, where Tom Holland, in a beige button-up and maroon 'BERO' apron, speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, recounting how his dad initially thought his idea wasn't a good one, and later worried about him becoming "too famous too quickly." Simultaneously, Gordon Ramsay, wearing a black t-shirt, is actively assembling a dish on a wooden cutting board, layering shredded cabbage, sauce, and fried chicken onto buns. Tom continues to explain his dad's desire for him to maintain his childhood by not doing "extra TV shows." Gordon interjects with a comment about the massive viewership of Tom's "lip-syncing battle," stating, "it's annoying because only 177 million people saw that," to which Tom humorously agrees, calling it "ridiculous." Tom then emphasizes that despite his other work, the "lip-syncing battle is the thing that everyone brings up," never his "Spider-Man" movies, adding, "It haunts me." Gordon laughs heartily, calling it a "viral sensation, mate, beyond belief," and praises Tom's "great job." A title card briefly appears, branding the segment as 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay' and 'SPONSORED BY HEXCLAD.' Gordon then asks about "those fishnets," prompting Tom to reveal they are "framed actually at the Met right now." Gordon picks up one of the gold 'BERO' cans visible on the counter, and the two men share a moment of shared amusement, with Gordon laughing and Tom playfully gesturing, maintaining the dynamic and friendly atmosphere of the cooking demonstration.

The video opens with a close-up of two gourmet chicken sandwiches on a wooden cutting board, featuring crispy fried chicken, shredded cabbage, and sauce, secured with skewers. Gordon Ramsay's voice immediately sets a lively tone, exclaiming, "Let's get straight to the conversation, we didn't even talk about this freaking delicious sandwich," to which Tom Holland replies, "We threw that together so quickly." The scene transitions to Gordon Ramsay, in a black t-shirt, animatedly describing the sandwich's components, holding a green vegetable, likely a lime or herb, in a modern stainless steel kitchen. He details the "bib lettuce," "butter lettuce," "chicken's been dredged twice" for crispiness, "tender" chicken, "hot sauce," and "slaw with a little jalapeño, bound with a light mayo," concluding with "and Bob's your uncle." Tom Holland, wearing a beige shirt and a maroon apron with 'BERO' printed on it, initially observes with arms crossed, then engages, remarking, "It looks delicious. It looks gorgeous." Gordon adds, "I love the way you squash it down like that and it just makes it all nice and congealed," followed by playful banter about eating in a car. The two men then stand side-by-side, with Gordon holding a sandwich and Tom taking a bite, both reacting with audible "mm" sounds and exclamations of delight, with Tom declaring, "Oh my word!" and "That is delicious." Tom further praises, "I think the slaw really brings it together to be honest," and confirms the chicken is "gorgeous." Gordon then picks up a gold-colored BERO can from the counter, while Tom continues to eat and gesture. The segment concludes with both men standing together in the professional kitchen, smiling and addressing the camera, with Gordon inviting viewers to "head to berobrewing.com to find out where you can pick up." The counter displays various cooking tools, ingredients, and multiple BERO cans. A title card briefly appears, reading 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay' and 'SPONSORED BY HEXCLAD', featuring a stylized egg yolk splash design, branding the segment. The overall atmosphere is lively and interactive, emphasizing food tasting and collaboration.

event_blocks.summary: In a modern, stainless steel kitchen, Gordon Ramsay, identifiable by his spiky blonde hair and black t-shirt, converses with Tom Holland, who sports curly brown hair, a beige button-up shirt, and a maroon apron. Ramsay comments on an 'amazing beer,' likely referring to the BERO cans present, and prompts viewers to 'subscribe for more amazing videos,' adding, 'James, it's always a pleasure,' and the phrase 'shaken, not stirred.' Holland, initially observing with crossed arms, laughs heartily as Ramsay turns to him, and they share a warm hug, signifying a positive interaction. The scene then shifts to a dynamic title card displaying 'SCRAMBLED with Gordon Ramsay' in bold yellow text, sponsored by HEXCLAD, with a stylized egg yolk splash. An energetic, upbeat musical theme, commencing with a 'whoosh' sound effect, accompanies this title card, set against the backdrop of a professional kitchen environment complete with appliances, shelves of bottles, and ingredients.
